papers.title_en: Boundedness of Left Half-Plane Eigenvalues for Non-Selfadjoint Indefinite Sturm--Liouville Problems with Applications to Fourier Modal Methods
papers.evidence: 针对层状光栅衍射问题的傅里叶模态方法的数学结果，与衍射光波导分析相关。
papers.tldr: 本文研究了一类非自伴不定Sturm-Liouville问题，证明其左半平面特征值有界，从而保证不稳定模态数量有限。这一结果直接适用于层状光栅衍射的傅里叶模态方法，为衍射光波导等周期结构的严格数值模拟提供了数学基础，有助于提升增强现实近眼显示中光栅设计的可靠性和效率。
papers.abstract_en: We study a general class of non-selfadjoint indefinite Sturm--Liouville problems of the form $$ -(p\,y')' + q\,y = λ\, p\, y, $$ on a finite interval with complex-valued coefficients, where $p$ is piecewise in $W^{2,\infty}$, non-vanishing, and satisfies a non-degenerate interface condition, and $q$ is bounded. We prove that all eigenvalues in the open left half-plane are contained in a bounded set, which, by classical Sturm--Liouville theory, implies their finiteness. A prominent instance of this class arises in the lamellar grating diffraction problem with transverse-magnetic (TM) polarization, where $p=ε(x)^{-1}$ is the inverse of a spatially varying permittivity profile. Our result provides a simple and rigorous criterion for identifying non-physical spurious modes in low-loss metallic gratings -- a notorious source of instability in Fourier modal methods. Numerical examples illustrate the practical utility of the criterion.

papers.title_en: AmbientEye: A Dataset for Pupil Segmentation under Natural Ambient Infrared Illumination
papers.authors: Mingyu Han, Hyunyoung Han, Nitheekulawatn Thommakoon, Gangtae Park, Jieun Han, Xucong Zhang, Ian Oakley
papers.evidence: 提供自然环境红外光照下的瞳孔分割数据集，支持智能眼镜被动眼动追踪，可用于基于瞳孔直径和环境光的感知亮度模型。
papers.tldr: 现有眼动追踪依赖主动红外照明，功耗问题限制户外应用。本研究探索仅用被动红外相机在自然阳光下实现瞳孔检测的可行性。我们构建了AmbientEye数据集，含260万张户外自然光眼部图像，并基准测试发现分割性能从受控红外的0.928降至0.767。该数据集为这一未探索的实用场景提供首个基准，推动全天候智能眼镜发展。
papers.abstract_en: Eye tracking is essential for smart glasses, as it provides insight into user attention for ambient intelligence applications. However, most existing eye-tracking systems rely on active infrared (IR) illumination, creating practical barriers to all-day outdoor use due to power consumption. In this paper, we investigate whether passive IR cameras alone, without any active IR light source, can enable reliable pupil detection in unconstrained outdoor environments, where ambient sunlight serves as the sole illumination source. To support this investigation, we introduce AmbientEye, a large-scale dataset of 2,606,225 eye images collected from 35 participants from 19 countries. It is captured outdoors under natural sunlight with two off-axis camera configurations and two sun-orientation conditions. We provide high-quality pupil annotation through SAM2 automatic segmentation, followed by refinement by human annotators. We benchmark a state-of-the-art pupil segmentation algorithm on our dataset and compare its performance with that on existing datasets under controlled IR illumination. Results reveal a substantial drop in pupil segmentation performance from 0.928 on controlled IR datasets to 0.767 on AmbientEye. This performance gap highlights the challenge of the ambient-light setting. This positions AmbientEye as a first benchmark for an unexplored and highly practical eye-tracking scenario.
